questions.question: Explain System Design Troubleshooting Part 4C and its primary use case in System Design.
questions.answer: System Design Troubleshooting Part 4C provides a structured mechanism to handle core application logic cleanly.

questions.question: What are common mistakes developers make with System Design Troubleshooting Part 4C?
questions.answer: Common issues include scoping errors, unhandled boundary cases, and improper resource cleanup.

questions.question: How does the System Design runtime optimize System Design Troubleshooting Part 4C under the hood?
questions.answer: The runtime engine uses symbol tables, call stack frames, and bytecode optimization to run instructions efficiently.
